structural_claim: Entropy serves as both a geometric property of horizons and a measure of information/microscopic states, with the same mathematical relationship S = A/4 (in Planck units) emerging from independent gravitational and statistical mechanical considerations

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Black hole entropy is proportional to horizon area, not volume, establishing entropy as a geometric property of causal boundaries
Quantum entanglement entropy scales with boundary area rather than volume in gapped systems, revealing entropy as fundamentally tied to boundaries
Entropy measures missing information, providing the information-theoretic foundation that connects to black hole information content
